title: Labelled vulnerable/safe benchmark corpus + measurement harness (verified, not asserted)
description: Make detection quality measurable rather than asserted. Author a small labelled corpus of vulnerable/safe code fixtures across the key money/credential/injection/supply-chain classes, plus a deterministic measurement harness that runs the finder-only path vs the finder+verifier path over the corpus and reports detection retention (recall on the vulnerable set) and false-positive-block reduction (on the safe set). This delivers architecture success criterion #3.

successCriteria.description: A benchmark corpus exists under a stable dir (e.g. src/orchestrator/security-knowledge/benchmark/fixtures/ or test-fixtures/security-benchmark/) with labelled cases: each case is a small code fixture + a JSON label {expected: 'vulnerable'|'safe', vulnClass?, signatureId?, stack} covering at least 12 vulnerable and 12 safe cases across iGaming money-integrity (TOCTOU, client-odds), dex-backend (withdrawal race, decimals, hot-wallet key), injection (SQLi, command, SSRF), supply-chain (malicious postinstall), and access-control (BOLA).

A measurement harness (pure/deterministic, no live LLM) accepts a pluggable 'finder' and 'verifier' function so it can be driven by injected fakes in CI; it computes, over the corpus: vulnerable-set recall (fraction of vulnerable cases flagged critical) and safe-set false-positive-block rate (fraction of safe cases flagged critical), for finder-only vs finder+verifier.

Using deterministic injected fakes that model the verifier disproving a labelled subset of the finder's safe-case false positives while confirming true vulnerable-case criticals, the harness demonstrates finder+verifier has a STRICTLY LOWER safe-set false-positive-block rate than finder-only AND equal-or-higher vulnerable-set recall (detection retained) — the measured realization of architecture success criterion #3.

Every vulnerable fixture's declared vulnClass/signatureId corresponds to an actual signature present in the relevant skills/bober.security-*/SKILL.md (a test cross-checks labels against the parsed index), so the corpus is grounded in the shipped knowledge rather than arbitrary.

The harness and corpus are runnable offline in CI (no network, no live provider); an optional documented command/flag allows running it against a real provider locally, but the required CI test uses injected fakes. npm run build + typecheck + lint pass; full suite green.

nonGoals: Do not require a live LLM in CI (the required test path uses deterministic injected finder/verifier fakes).
Do not gate the pipeline on benchmark thresholds (this is a measurement harness, not a new blocking gate).
Do not author an exhaustive corpus — a small, representative, grounded set is the goal.
stopConditions: The harness deterministically shows FP-block reduction with retained recall using injected fakes.
Every vulnerable label maps to a real shipped signature.
definitionOfDone: A grounded labelled vulnerable/safe corpus and a deterministic, offline measurement harness exist and demonstrate the finder+verifier pipeline reduces false-positive blocks while retaining detection, delivering the architecture's measurable success criterion, with the suite green.
assumptions: The finder and verifier can be represented as injectable function types so the harness is deterministic in CI; the real runSecurityAudit/runSecurityVerifier satisfy those shapes.
Corpus fixtures are small illustrative snippets, not runnable apps; labels reference the vulnClass/signatureId taxonomy shipped in sprints 1-4.
This corpus doubles as few-shot exemplar material and as a regression guard for future signature edits.
generatorNotes: (1) fixtures: small files (e.g. .ts/.sol snippets) + a manifest JSON listing {file, expected, stack, vulnClass?, signatureId?}. Draw vulnerable/safe PAIRS from the unsafeExample/safeExample already in the skill files where possible (keeps them grounded). (2) harness.ts: pure function measure(corpus, finderFn, verifierFn) -> {finderOnly:{recall,fpBlockRate}, finderPlusVerifier:{recall,fpBlockRate}}. finderFn/verifierFn are injected; for CI, provide deterministic fakes: finderFake flags a case critical iff its label is vulnerable OR it is in a fixed 'false-positive' safe subset; verifierFake disproves the safe subset and confirms vulnerable ones. (3) test asserts finderPlusVerifier.fpBlockRate < finderOnly.fpBlockRate AND finderPlusVerifier.recall >= finderOnly.recall. (4) label-grounding test: parse the index, assert each vulnerable label's signatureId/vulnClass exists. (5) Optionally add a documented `describe.skip`/env-gated block that wires the real finder+verifier for local manual runs. Keep CI offline/deterministic.
evaluatorNotes: sc-9-1: count vulnerable>=12 and safe>=12 across the named classes. sc-9-2/9-3: run the harness with the deterministic fakes and assert the strict FP-block reduction + retained recall inequalities; make sure the harness itself is deterministic (no Math.random/Date). sc-9-4: cross-check every vulnerable label against the parsed SecurityKnowledgeIndex. sc-9-5: confirm no network/live-provider in the required test; build+typecheck+lint+full suite.
